food_subgroup: Chicken-based soup meal with avocado and Caesar dressing

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for avocado Caesar chicken soup meal specifically. FOODON_00002427 (chicken soup food product) is the closest defensible parent. The avocado and Caesar dressing components are not captured by this code.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Soups and broths) is the closest parent category. No specific code exists for avocado Caesar chicken soup meal as a composite restaurant dish.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT clinical food concept found for this composite restaurant meal product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is approximately 450 g, representing a full meal set comprising a bowl of avocado Caesar chicken soup with croutons, Parmesan, and Caesar dressing, as typically served at Subway Singapore or similar fast-casual outlets.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated composite from USDA FoodData Central: Chicken noodle soup (SR Legacy 06016), Avocado raw (SR Legacy 09037), Caesar salad dressing (SR Legacy 04135), and Croutons (SR Legacy 18239)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA Branded Foods, or any primary regional database for 'Avocado Caesar Chicken Soup Meal' as sold at Subway Singapore. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries: chicken soup base (~55% by weight, ~100 kcal/100g), avocado (~15% by weight, ~160 kcal/100g), Caesar dressing (~10% by weight, ~360 kcal/100g), croutons (~5% by weight, ~400 kcal/100g), and romaine lettuce/Parmesan (~15% by weight, ~20 kcal/100g). Weighted blend yields approximately 145 kcal/100g. All component values sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. Confidence is low due to restaurant-specific formulation variability.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of mixed meals and soups — analogous estimate based on chicken vegetable soup (GI ~40) and Caesar salad components; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Avocado Caesar Chicken Soup Meal. GI estimated at 45 based on: (1) chicken vegetable soup GI ~40 (University of Sydney GI Database, tested food), (2) avocado has negligible glycemic contribution (GI ~15, very low carbohydrate), (3) Caesar dressing has minimal carbohydrate, (4) croutons raise GI slightly (GI ~70 for white bread croutons, small proportion). Weighted composite estimate yields GI ~45 (low category). GL per serving = (45 × 9.8 × 450/100) / 100 = 19.8. Estimation basis documented per Veritas v2 GI policy.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~420 mg/100g; ~1890 mg per full serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this meal due to its very high sodium load (~1890 mg per serving, representing approximately 82% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2300 mg), primarily from Caesar dressing, chicken broth, and Parmesan cheese; portion reduction or requesting low-sodium broth is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~19.8 per serving) with low GI (~45) but significant carbohydrate from croutons
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes can consume this meal with caution; the low GI (~45) from avocado and protein-rich chicken moderates postprandial glucose response, but the crouton component raises the glycemic load to moderate levels (~19.8 per serving), so portion control and omitting or reducing croutons is recommended to better manage blood glucose.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium and moderate saturated fat, partially offset by heart-healthy monounsaturated fats from avocado
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, the high sodium content (~1890 mg per serving) is a primary concern for blood pressure management, while the avocado component provides beneficial monounsaturated fatty acids (~4.1 g/100g) that may support lipid profiles; overall the meal should be consumed occasionally and with sodium-reduction modifications.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Avocado Caesar Chicken Soup Meal is a Western-style fast-casual meal set sold at Subway Singapore outlets and similar venues, combining a warm chicken soup base with avocado, Caesar dressing, romaine lettuce, croutons, and Parmesan cheese. The dish merges the creamy, rich flavour profile of a classic Caesar salad — anchovy-based dressing, sharp Parmesan, crunchy croutons — with the warming, savoury character of a chicken broth soup. The avocado contributes a buttery texture and heart-healthy monounsaturated fats, while the chicken provides lean protein (~9.5 g/100g). The meal is moderately energy-dense at approximately 145 kcal/100g and carries a low glycemic index (~45) due to the high fat and protein content moderating glucose absorption, though the glycemic load per full serving is moderate (~19.8) owing to the crouton component. Sodium content is high (~420 mg/100g; ~1890 mg per serving), making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The meal is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those with gluten, dairy, egg, or fish (anchovy) allergies. It is a filling, protein-forward lunch or dinner option popular in Singapore's Western fast-casual dining segment, priced at approximately SGD 15.90.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for 'Avocado Caesar Chicken Soup Meal' exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central branded, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are estimated by weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy component entries. GI is an analogous estimate. Restaurant-specific formulation at Subway Singapore or Funan Mix & Match may differ materially from these estimates. Confidence is therefore low.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component entries (chicken soup, avocado, Caesar dressing, croutons, romaine, Parmesan); no exact branded entry was found for this specific meal. 2. Vitamin B1, B2, B3, B6, B12, folate, vitamin D, vitamin E, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, and selenium are set to -1 as these micronutrients are not reported in the composite SR Legacy sources used for estimation and cannot be reliably derived without a direct laboratory analysis of the specific restaurant product. 3. GI is an analogous estimate (GI ~45) based on chicken vegetable soup (University of Sydney GI Database) and avocado/Caesar dressing component GI values; no direct GI measurement exists for this meal. 4. Halal status is listed as false/unknown because Caesar dressing typically contains anchovy (fish), and the halal certification status of the specific Subway Singapore or Funan Mix & Match outlet was not confirmed from an approved source. 5. The food_name has been canonicalized from 'Avo Caesar Chicken Soup Meal' to 'Avocado Caesar Chicken Soup Meal' for clarity; 'Avo Caesar Chicken Soup Meal' is retained as the primary English alias. 6. Multilingual names are provided for Singaporean cuisine context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) per Veritas v2 multilingual rules. 7. Serving size of 450 g is an estimate for a full meal set; actual weight may vary by outlet.
